all.en: Last spring, I visited a small coastal town during their annual temple festival.
all.zh: 去年春天，我在一個沿海小鎮造訪了當地一年一度的廟會。

all.en: My friend's grandmother insisted that I join their religious procession.
all.zh: 朋友的奶奶堅持要我一起參加他們的宗教遶境活動。

all.en: The narrow streets were decorated with red lanterns and colorful flags.
all.zh: 狹窄的街道上掛滿了紅燈籠和五彩繽紛的旗幟。

all.en: Firecrackers exploded loudly, filling the air with smoke and excitement.
all.zh: 鞭炮聲此起彼落，空氣中瀰漫著煙霧與興奮的氛圍。

all.en: Eight men carried a heavy wooden palanquin on their strong shoulders.
all.zh: 八個男人用厚實的肩膀，扛著一頂沉重的木製神轎。

all.en: Inside the palanquin was a beautifully decorated statue of the sea goddess.
all.zh: 神轎內安奉著裝飾精美的海神娘娘神像。

all.en: Drums and gongs created a steady rhythm that echoed off the buildings.
all.zh: 鼓聲與鑼聲交織出穩定的節奏，在建築之間迴盪。

all.en: Locals knelt at their doorways and offered fruit, tea, and incense sticks.
all.zh: 當地居民跪在自家門口，獻上水果、茶水和香。

all.en: My friend's grandmother explained the long history behind every ritual.
all.zh: 朋友的奶奶向我解釋每一項儀式背後悠久的歷史。

all.en: I felt deeply moved by the strong sense of community in the town.
all.zh: 小鎮中濃厚的社區情感，深深地打動了我。

all.en: It reminded me that faith and tradition still shape modern Taiwanese life.
all.zh: 這讓我體會到，信仰與傳統仍深深影響著現代台灣人的生活。

all.en: After the procession, the temple square filled with families sharing traditional snacks.
all.zh: 遶境結束後，廟埕擠滿了一起分享傳統小吃的家庭。

all.en: My friend's grandmother bought us each a steaming bowl of oyster vermicelli.
all.zh: 朋友的奶奶為我們每人買了一碗熱騰騰的蚵仔麵線。

all.en: Children ran around with sparklers while elders chatted under colorful canopies.
all.zh: 小朋友拿著仙女棒到處跑，長輩則在彩色帳棚下閒聊。

all.en: A puppet show began on a small stage near the main temple entrance.
all.zh: 主廟入口附近的小舞台上，開始上演了一場布袋戲。

all.en: I struggled to follow the dialogue, but the lively music kept me entertained.
all.zh: 我聽不太懂對白，但生動的配樂讓我看得津津有味。

all.en: My friend explained that the story dated back at least two hundred years.
all.zh: 朋友解釋說，這個故事至少有兩百年的歷史。

all.en: Later, we visited the temple to offer incense and pray for safe travels.
all.zh: 後來，我們進到廟裡上香，祈求旅途平安。

all.en: The grandmother taught me the proper way to bow three times respectfully.
all.zh: 奶奶教我恭敬地三鞠躬的正確方式。

all.en: On the train back to Taipei, I wrote down everything I had learned.
all.zh: 回台北的火車上，我把當天學到的一切都記了下來。

all.en: That small coastal town had quietly become my favorite memory of the year.
all.zh: 那個沿海小鎮，悄悄成了我那年最珍貴的回憶。

all.en: Six months later, my friend invited me back for the autumn temple festival.
all.zh: 六個月後，朋友邀請我回去參加秋天的廟會。

all.en: This time I prepared by reading several books about Taiwanese folk religion.
all.zh: 這次我事先讀了幾本關於台灣民間信仰的書，做好功課。

all.en: His grandmother seemed delighted that I had taken her culture so seriously.
all.zh: 他奶奶很高興看到，我這麼認真看待她的文化。

all.en: She introduced me to several temple elders during the morning preparations.
all.zh: 早上的準備工作期間，她介紹了好幾位廟裡的長輩讓我認識。

all.en: They handed me a small flag to carry during this year's procession route.
all.zh: 他們交給我一面小旗子，讓我在今年的遶境隊伍中扛著。

all.en: I felt honored and surprisingly nervous about playing such a respectful role.
all.zh: 能擔任如此莊重的角色，我感到很榮幸，也意外地緊張。

all.en: My footsteps matched the steady drum rhythm throughout the long quiet streets.
all.zh: 我的步伐配合著穩定的鼓聲，走過漫長安靜的街道。

all.en: Locals greeted me warmly as if I had always belonged to their town.
all.zh: 當地居民熱情地跟我打招呼，彷彿我一直都是他們鎮上的一員。

all.en: After the procession, the grandmother told me she considered me her godson.
all.zh: 遶境結束後，奶奶告訴我，她已經把我當成她的乾兒子了。

all.en: Faith, I learned, could open doors that no map had ever shown me.
all.zh: 我體會到，信仰能為我打開許多地圖從未指出過的門。

all.en: The next spring, the grandmother invited me to her granddaughter's traditional wedding.
all.zh: 隔年春天，奶奶邀請我參加她孫女的傳統婚禮。

all.en: I arrived early wearing a respectful dark suit and carrying a thoughtful red envelope.
all.zh: 我提早抵達，穿著得體的深色西裝，帶著一個用心準備的紅包。

all.en: The temple ceremony lasted nearly two hours with chants and gentle incense smoke.
all.zh: 廟裡的儀式持續了將近兩小時，伴隨著誦經聲和裊裊香煙。

all.en: The bride's family treated me as if I were truly one of their own.
all.zh: 新娘的家人待我如同真正的自家人。

all.en: I shared a banquet table with three temple elders I had met during processions.
all.zh: 我和三位曾在遶境時認識的廟裡長輩同桌共享喜宴。

all.en: They asked careful questions about my work and offered surprisingly wise life advice.
all.zh: 他們仔細詢問我的工作，並提供了出乎意料睿智的人生建議。

all.en: Toward the evening, the grandmother quietly placed a small jade pendant around my neck.
all.zh: 傍晚時分，奶奶悄悄地把一塊小玉墜戴在我脖子上。

all.en: She whispered that it had protected three generations of men in her family.
all.zh: 她輕聲說，這塊玉曾保佑過她家三代的男丁。

all.en: I promised to wear it carefully and visit the coastal town more often.
all.zh: 我答應會好好佩戴，並更常回那個沿海小鎮探望她。

all.en: A single procession, I realized, had given me an entire second family of love.
all.zh: 我體會到，一次遶境，竟為我帶來了第二個充滿愛的家庭。
